title: Immigration Compliance Services | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Immigration Compliance Services

### Maximize your immigration compliance.
What’s the best way to solve your immigration compliance issues?
Simple: Prevent them from happening in the first place.
The problem is, we live in the real world—and moving talent across borders is anything but simple—especially now.
And while immigration compliance is growing more and more vital to business operations worldwide, it’s challenging to avoid the wide variety of issues that can arise. Complex processes like corporate restructuring, large-scale projects, back-to-back rotational assignments and travel bans can be especially challenging.
Successfully navigating immigration compliance can mean the difference between growing your business or obstructing your goals. Fragomen strategically partners with you to provide advisory and immigration compliance services that help you:
- Manage your most complicated immigration projects and scenarios Craft compliance solutions that avoid pitfalls before they happen Help correct issues if they do occur
- Manage your most complicated immigration projects and scenarios
- Craft compliance solutions that avoid pitfalls before they happen
- Help correct issues if they do occur
In short, we can help you maximize your company’s immigration compliance.

### Prevent and correct immigration compliance issues—before they become a problem
Early detection and swift correction of compliance oversights is crucial to avoiding negative consequences.
Internal Immigration Audit and Training
Through proactive internal audits, Fragomen helps to correct errors before they become problems. We conduct compliance and file reviews and can help you implement process improvements. We can also raise your compliance profile by training your mobility staff to spot and address potential issues up front.
Government Immigration Audit or Investigation Assistance
In the current political environment, government immigration audits and investigations are increasing in many countries worldwide. Fragomen’s Government Strategies and Compliance team has been guiding and representing organizations during government site visits, external audits and investigations for decades. We know how to demonstrate that immigration practices are compliant and properly documented.

### Strategic immigration compliance partnering and advice for all your complex projects
When the movement or acquisition of talent is tied into large corporate projects, you face a unique set of immigration compliance challenges. Working with an experienced advisor up front can help streamline the process and avoid compliance roadblocks along the way.
M&As and Corporate Restructuring
Mergers and acquisitions (M&A), divestures and other types of corporate restructuring activities are typically focused on the “big-picture” and on mitigating financial risks—and the critical area of immigration compliance is often overlooked. If you’re not considering the immigration impacts of a transaction, it can lead to serious repercussions for employees and the success of the merger. Fragomen can partner with you to provide strategic guidance and support on the immigration aspects of your corporate activities.
Large scale projects around the world often require the simultaneous movement of dozens to hundreds of employees. They face different challenges based on how many employees are moving, their roles and credentials, and the countries involved.
- Execute project needs through compliant—yet practical—immigration solutions Maintain compliance with local immigration-related processes and regulations Build custom action plans in line with your priorities and targets, helping you to stay on track and achieve your goals
- Execute project needs through compliant—yet practical—immigration solutions
- Maintain compliance with local immigration-related processes and regulations
- Build custom action plans in line with your priorities and targets, helping you to stay on track and achieve your goals
Rotational and Back-to-Back Assignments
The use of short-term assignments—such as rotational and multiple or back-to-back assignments helps to control costs and build your global operations. However, the rapid deployment of employees to foreign countries can also lead to a host of immigration compliance concerns.
We help hundreds of companies around the world:
- Manage the immigration compliance risks of short-term cross-border travel and understand its intricacies and challenges Design proactive strategies for avoiding common pitfalls
- Manage the immigration compliance risks of short-term cross-border travel and understand its intricacies and challenges
- Design proactive strategies for avoiding common pitfalls
- Incorporate immigration compliance into your overall plan
- Incorporate immigration compliance into your overall plan

### Navigate a changing world
EU Integrated Compliance and Advisory Services
We provide compliant yet pragmatic advice on many issues impacting mobility in the EU, including integrated work readiness services such as the requirements associated with the EU Posted Workers Directive and international social security certificates of coverage.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
